skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec.
skill.summary: Transform React/Remotion TSX code into rendered MP4 videos via the inference.sh platform.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The 32 'external_commands' detections are markdown code block examples in documentation, not actual shell execution. The 5 'network' findings are documentation URLs. The 3 'blocker' findings are false positives from frontmatter text. The skill uses restricted Bash access (infsh CLI only) for legitimate video rendering via inference.sh platform.

content.user_title: Render videos from Remotion React code
content.value_statement: Creating videos programmatically requires complex rendering infrastructure. This skill transforms your React/Remotion TSX components into MP4 videos instantly via the inference.sh platform.
actual_capabilities: Render React/Remotion TSX components to MP4 video files
Support all Remotion APIs including useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, and interpolate
Configure video resolution, frame rate, duration, and codec settings
Create animated sequences using Remotion Sequence component
Pass custom props to Remotion components for dynamic content
Stream rendering progress updates during video generation
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (infsh) to be installed and authenticated
Video rendering depends on inference.sh platform availability
Component code must be valid TSX with a default export
No direct access to local file system for assets - use URLs

use_cases.title: Automated Video Generation
use_cases.description: Generate branded video content programmatically from templates with dynamic text, colors, and data visualizations.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams creating personalized video campaigns at scale

use_cases.title: Animated Data Visualizations
use_cases.description: Transform data into animated explainer videos showing trends, comparisons, and insights with smooth transitions.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts presenting insights through engaging video content

use_cases.title: UI Animation Prototyping
use_cases.description: Prototype and share UI animations as shareable video files for design reviews and stakeholder presentations.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers and designers iterating on motion design

prompt_templates.title: Simple Fade Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5-second 1080p video with white text fading in on a dark background saying 'Hello World' using Remotion's interpolate function for opacity animation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Basic text animation with fade-in effect

prompt_templates.title: Animated Counter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 3-second square video showing a percentage counter animating from 0% to 100% with smooth number transitions using useCurrentFrame and useVideoConfig hooks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Using frame-based animations and video config

prompt_templates.title: Spring Physics Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 2-second animation of a bouncing ball using Remotion's spring API with custom damping and stiffness values. Use a colorful background and export at 60fps.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Sequence Title Card
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 6-second title sequence with three text elements appearing in sequence. First show the title at frame 0, subtitle at frame 30, and logo at frame 60. Each element should fade in over 15 frames. Use 1920x1080 resolution.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Sequence-based multi-element animation with precise timing

output_examples.input: TSX component with fade-in text animation, 3 seconds, 1080p
Animated counter from 0-100% with props for custom start/end values
output_examples.output: Rendered square video showing smooth percentage animation with progress indicator

best_practices: Keep component code focused and minimal - complex logic increases render time
Use interpolate for smooth animations instead of manual frame calculations
Test animations at lower resolution first before rendering final high-quality output
Provide explicit width, height, fps, and duration for consistent results
anti_patterns: Do not include external API calls or async operations in Remotion components
Avoid importing packages beyond remotion and react - they may not be available
Do not rely on local file paths for assets - use HTTP URLs instead
Avoid extremely long durations or high frame counts that timeout rendering

faq.question: What video formats and codecs are supported?
faq.answer: The skill outputs MP4 files. Common codecs include H.264 for broad compatibility and ProRes for high-quality master files. Specify via the codec parameter.

faq.question: Can I use images or videos inside my Remotion composition?
faq.answer: Yes, use Remotion's Img and Video components with HTTP URLs for external assets. Local file paths are not accessible.

faq.question: How do I pass dynamic data to my video component?
faq.answer: Use the 'props' parameter in your input. Your component should accept props as the second parameter: export default function Main({ title, color })

faq.question: What is the maximum video duration I can render?
faq.answer: Duration depends on the inference.sh platform limits. For longer videos, consider breaking into segments or using streaming mode for progress feedback.

faq.question: Can I preview the video before final rendering?
faq.answer: Yes, render at lower resolution (e.g., 640x360) and shorter duration first to verify the animation, then render the final version at full quality.

faq.question: Do I need a Remotion Pro license to use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill uses the inference.sh platform which handles Remotion licensing. You can use Remotion APIs without managing licenses directly.
